Module com.example.cincuentazo
Package com.example.cincuentazo.concurrency

Class MachineTurnService

java.lang.Object
com.example.cincuentazo.concurrency.MachineTurnService
public class MachineTurnService extends Object
Service that schedules the timed actions of a machine player using background threads, so the user interface never freezes.

The game rules require a machine to take between 2 and 4 seconds to play a card, and between 1 and 2 seconds to draw a card. These waits are executed on separate threads by means of JavaFX Tasks. When a task finishes, the provided callback is run safely on the JavaFX Application Thread through Platform.runLater(java.lang.Runnable).

This class is responsible only for the timing/concurrency aspect; the actual game decisions are passed in as callbacks by the controller.

    Description

    Maximum extra milliseconds (random) before drawing a card.

    Method

    private void
    runAfterDelay(int delayMs, Runnable action)
    Runs the given action after the specified delay on a background thread, then dispatches the action to the JavaFX Application Thread.
    void
    scheduleDraw(Runnable onDraw)
    Schedules the "draw a card" action of a machine after a random delay of 1 to 2 seconds.
    void
    schedulePlay(Runnable onPlay)
    Schedules the "play a card" action of a machine after a random delay of 2 to 4 seconds.

  • Constructor Details

    • MachineTurnService

      public MachineTurnService()

  • Method Details

    • schedulePlay

      public void schedulePlay(Runnable onPlay)
      Schedules the "play a card" action of a machine after a random delay of 2 to 4 seconds. The action runs on the JavaFX Application Thread.
      Parameters:
      onPlay - the action to execute once the delay elapses

    • scheduleDraw

      public void scheduleDraw(Runnable onDraw)
      Schedules the "draw a card" action of a machine after a random delay of 1 to 2 seconds. The action runs on the JavaFX Application Thread.
      Parameters:
      onDraw - the action to execute once the delay elapses

    • runAfterDelay

      private void runAfterDelay(int delayMs, Runnable action)
      Runs the given action after the specified delay on a background thread, then dispatches the action to the JavaFX Application Thread.
      Parameters:
      delayMs - the delay in milliseconds
      action - the action to run after the delay
